fields.name: Executable Name
fields.description: Name of the Executable.

fields.name: Executable API Name
fields.description: API Name for the Executable. If left blank, it automatically defaults to the Executable's Name when the record is saved.

fields.name: Pipeline
fields.description: The Pipeline this Executable belongs to. Pipelines group related Executables so you can navigate, filter, and reorder them together. Within a Pipeline, Executables run or display in the order set by their sequence number.

fields.name: Direction
fields.description: Defines the source and target of this Executable's data flow — where records are read from and actioned to.

fields.name: Seq No.
fields.description: Sequence number of this Executable within its Pipeline, determining the order of batch executions, triggers, data lists, and action buttons.

fields.name: Owner
fields.description: The user who owns this Executable.

fields.name: Deployable?
fields.description: Indicates whether this Executable can be deployed to other orgs (e.g., sandbox to production).

fields.name: Raw Record
fields.description: Opens a full field inspector for this record, showing every field's API name, type, and value, with quick search, edit, and clone.

fields.name: All Internal Users Have Read Access?
fields.description: When enabled, DSP creates a sharing record granting all internal users read access to the current Executable; this field is automatically turned on when #Trigger, #Data List, or #Action Button is enabled and can be disabled manually to revoke that access.

fields.name: Origin ID
fields.description: Identifier used for matching records during cross-system synchronization. It defaults to the Salesforce record ID on save but can be overridden with a custom unique value. Once established, it must remain unchanged after the initial sync.

fields.name: Description
fields.description: A brief description of the Executable's purpose and scope.

fields.name: Specification
fields.description: Free-form field for documenting detailed information about the Executable, including its configurations, underlying rationale, and any additional notes.

fields.name: #Batch
fields.description: Enables this Executable to run as a batch job — scheduled or on-demand.

fields.name: #Trigger
fields.description: Enables this Executable to run as a trigger.

fields.name: #Data List
fields.description: Enables this Executable to run as a Data List.

fields.name: #Action Button
fields.description: Enables this Executable to run as an Action Button.

fields.name: #Data Loader
fields.description: Enables this Executable to run as a Data Loader — for reusable, transformable CSV imports.

fields.name: Source Object Name
fields.description: Name of the Source Object.

fields.name: Target Object Name
fields.description: Name of the Target Object.

fields.name: Target Matching Field
fields.description: API name of the target field (case-sensitive) used to identify matching target records. If multiple matches are found for a source record and the Principal Selection Rule is defined, only the principal record is selected—otherwise, all matches are used. Actions then execute based on these results (e.g. Insert is skipped when matches exist, Update runs only for matched records, and Merge consolidates secondary matches into the principal record).

fields.name: Action
fields.description: The action to be performed on the target records.
